description: A large area densely covered with trees and undergrowth, forming an interconnected ecosystem. 'Shelira' names the living cathedral where trees gather in community—not merely a collection of wood, but a breathing network where canopy shelters countless beings and roots share nutrients through fungal threads.
sound_symbolism: The word rustles through the trees. 'She' /ʃe/ begins with the whisper of wind through leaves. Then 'li' flows with the liquid life within, sap and streams. Finally 'ra' reaches upward, the canopy opening to light.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo shelira' (the forest). Takes the vegetal classifier 'lipha' when counted, treating the forest as a single living entity. Related to 'shiro' (tree) through initial fricative. Encompasses all woodland types from dense jungle to open woodland.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Forests are the lungs of the planet—carbon sinks, biodiversity havens, and models for sustainable community living.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional cultures recognized forests as sacred homes, sources of medicine, food, and spiritual connection.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The forest teaches interconnection—no tree stands alone, each depends on and supports the whole community.
